articles.title: Myopia now, glaucoma later? The hidden link explained and how to reduce your risk
articles.content: Instead, you might pay more attention to myopia or short-sightedness. But hold that thought because there is a link between the two eye conditions. In other words, having myopia now, especially severe myopia, may increase your risk of developing glaucoma later in life. About 57,800 Singaporeans aged 60 years and above have glaucoma, according to NHG Eye Institute. This number is projected to rise by 43 per cent, reaching 85,800 by 2040. Age certainly plays a part

articles.title: Eric Dane's Official Cause of Death Confirmed
articles.content: Eric Dane's official cause of death has been confirmed. The Grey's Anatomy and Euphoria star died on Thursday, Feb. Dane announced in April 2025 that he had been diagnosed with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS). The Grey's Anatomy and Euphoria actor died of respiratory failure, according to his death certificate obtained by PEOPLE. Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) is an underlying cause of death. His family confirmed that the star died on Thursday, Feb. 19, nearly one year after he announced he had been diagnosed with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS). There is no cure for ALS. However, there are medications and forms of...
